entries.evidence_summary: Authority is expressed as a strict operative precedence chain, with static docs separated under Reference evidence only.
Material pushback is defined once in <guardrails> and referenced consistently in freeze, terminal-state, and completion language.
Canonical promoted artifacts are separated from run-scoped evidence with explicit roots and lifecycle rules.
Frozen, pushback, blocked, and resume_required each have distinct, testable trigger predicates and artifact expectations.
Step 3 is limited to the self-check behavior that actually exists today and explicitly requires semantic keyword matching for out-of-scope coverage.
The not-in-scope list is deduplicated to one exclusion per concept and preserves the merged contract meaning without repeated entries.
Arbitration has a trigger condition, actor, evidence requirement, and bounded effect: minor-only disagreements, leader rationale, and no override of reject or material pushback.
All four required promoted artifacts are declared with full canonical tool-relative paths, and Step 2 requires contract package_files to match them.
Completion is defined separately for frozen, pushback, blocked, and resume_required, with explicit artifact predicates for each state.
Context gathering is explicitly labeled as preconditions before Step 1, and numbered execution steps begin in <steps>.
The draft explicitly requires carried-forward reviewer findings to be tracked by conceptual_group ID in the next fix items map.
Inputs and validation steps state the hard gate for reviewer_count, reviewer array length, and Codex+Claude reviewer-pair composition.
Decision-history preservation is mechanical: canonical decision log appends on frozen update/fix, canonical board summary replaces on freeze, and prior run summaries remain in run evidence.
Each section distinguishes current implementation from durable contract or target behavior, avoiding false claims about already-implemented features.
Round evidence is defined as per-round output, not revision-only output, and the artifact matrix distinguishes always-written evidence from conditional artifacts.
The outputs section acts as one authoritative artifact matrix, declaring class, full path or root, and exact lifecycle by operation and terminal state.
The draft inherits governance from the approved architecture: live governance first, docs as reference only, freeze on approved or conditional with no material pushback, and minor-only arbitration.
The role mission is enduring and role-level; it does not describe a specific resume, bootstrap, or round-specific invocation state.
The writable surface is explicitly bounded to the canonical role root, the current run root, and named governed subpaths and run-root artifacts.
Only operative governance sources appear in the authority chain; static docs are classified separately as reference evidence.
Board mechanics are source-backed: no Codex-only leader rule, no unsupported verdict names, and no invented arbitration power beyond minor-only disagreement handling.
Use-when bullets contain inclusions, and Not in scope contains exclusions only, with positive board-review ownership moved to <authority>.
